title: Bridge Essay: Dorsal Striatal Circuits for Habits, Compulsions and Addictions.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Mechanicality** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Mechanicality — habit as automated behavior, loss of conscious control, automaticity.

**Dorsal Striatal Circuits for Habits, Compulsions and Addictions.**. Front Syst Neurosci.. PMID: 31379523. DOI: 10.3389/fnsys.2019.00028. Here, we review the neural circuit bases of habits, compulsions, and addictions, behaviors which are all characterized by relatively automatic action performance. We discuss relevant studies, primarily from the rodent literature, and describe how major headway has been made in identifying the brain regions and neural cell types whose activity is modulated during the acquisition and performance of these automated behaviors.
